citation: Rapini, A. and Jordanous, A. (2024). Beat Tracking for Salsa Music: Adapting and Benchmarking Models Using a Newly Introduced Salsa Dataset. Proc. 1st Latin American Music Information Retrieval Workshop (LAMIR), ISMIR.
notes: Protocol: 5-fold CV on SalsaSet, 10% validation from training fold, best val-loss checkpoint, F-measure at +/-70ms tolerance (Rapini & Jordanous 2024 Section 4).

BockTCN architecture: 81-band log-magnitude spectrogram (12 bands/octave, 30-17kHz, 100fps), conv frontend (3x3+pool, 3x3+pool, 1x8 no pool), TCN with dilations 2^0..2^10, kernel 5, 16 filters. Training: Adam lr=1e-3, batch=1, binary cross-entropy, ReduceLROnPlateau factor=0.2, early stop patience=50. DBN decoding: BPM range 55-215, transition lambda=100. (Davies & Böck 2019 Table I).
Two-frame target shoulders (value=0.5) around annotated beat frames per Davies & Böck 2019 Section II-B1.
